summary: Matched a sweep of very small previously-unmatched functions, mostly 28-byte accessors, setters, predicates, and tiny state transitions across fighter, game-mode, stage, interface, utility, and sysdolphin baselib code. The PR also tightened prototypes and data types needed to express those functions cleanly, including Kirby ArticleDynamicBones and Item_GObj pointers, MatchEnd and Ground_GObj signatures, a Greens ground-vars bitfield struct, a small un_80301C64_t output struct, and a padded global layout for un_803FA258. The decomp.dev bot reported +568 matched bytes, 18 new matches, and one ftCo_CaptureKirby improvement.
what_changed: Implemented ftCommon small helper fn_800D769C as a motion-id adjuster: if ft->motion_id is at or after ftCo_MS_ItemScopeStartEmpty, return msid + 8, otherwise return msid.
Updated ftCo_CaptureKirby to call ftKb_SpecialN_800F5B20(fp->victim_gobj, &vec1), improving ftCo_800BD1DC according to the bot report.

Implemented Kirby helpers in ftKb_Init.c: ftKb_Init_800EEB00 writes a hat dynamic-bones pointer to an output parameter; ftKb_SpecialN_800F5B20 writes two SpecialN velocity fields into a Vec2 output; ftKb_SpecialNSk_80106020 returns ft->fv.kb.xB4 when gobj is non-NULL; ftKb_SpecialNIc_80108CE8 clears ft->fv.kb.xC0 if the passed item matches.
Corrected Kirby prototypes in ftKb_Init.h for ArticleDynamicBones**, Vec2* output, s32 return, and Item_GObj* parameters; added needed ft/forward.h and it/forward.h includes.
Typed ftKb_FighterVars.xC0 as Item_GObj* instead of UNK_T to support item-pointer comparisons and clearing.
Implemented gm_80168940 as a MatchEnd standings accessor conditioned on slot_type == 0, and implemented three gm_1601 global flag setters: gm_8016A434, fn_8016A450, and fn_8016A46C.
Implemented gm_8017E7E0 as a bool predicate returning whether lbl_80472C30.x7C equals 0x14.

Added grGreens_GroundVars with an x0_flags bitfield to the Ground union, introduced grGr_804D6AAD, and implemented fn_80214658 to clear gp->gv.greens.x0_flags.b0 and the static flag before returning the Ground pointer.
Changed Kongo and Old Kongo stage callbacks to take Ground_GObj* and implemented grKongo_801D8270 and grOldKongo_802105AC as tiny state transitions from gv.inishie2.xC6 == 1 to 2.
Implemented ifMagnify_802FC998 as an offscreen-state predicate for a player slot.
Added un_80301C64_t and implemented un_80301C64 to copy un_803FA258.x138 into arg0->x0 and zero arg0->x4; expanded the static un_803FA258 layout with padding and an x138 pointer field.
Implemented hsd_80396868 in baselib particle by declaring lbl_8040BC3C layout enough to update x10 with a bitmask expression: (x10 & 0x0FFFFFF0) | 0x80000000.
The decomp.dev bot reported 18 new matches, including sixteen +28 byte functions listed in the PR title scope plus un_80300218 and un_80300248 reaching 100%.
smart_moves: Converted UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and void* placeholders into precise signatures only where the matched code required them, letting small functions compile as normal typed C instead of relying on opaque macros.
Used output-parameter patterns for tiny functions that write values into caller-owned storage, notably ftKb_Init_800EEB00, ftKb_SpecialN_800F5B20, and un_80301C64.
Modeled bitfields and union members rather than using raw offsets for the Greens stage flag clear, making fn_80214658 readable and reusable.
Used type-specific GObj aliases such as Fighter_GObj*, Ground_GObj*, and Item_GObj* to make GET_FIGHTER/GET_GROUND patterns and pointer comparisons line up with the intended subsystem.
Matched tiny state-transition functions by expressing exactly the observable condition and assignment, for example Kongo and Old Kongo changing xC6 from 1 to 2.
For the particle global, declared only the observed external layout with padding up to x10, enough to support the matched bitmask write without overclaiming the full struct.
Kept unknown/address-derived names where semantics were still weak, while still improving types and prototypes around those symbols.
decomp_lessons: A batch of 28-byte functions can often be matched by looking for simple idioms: one conditional branch and return, one global bitfield assignment, one output-parameter store pair, or one conditional state update.
Small functions frequently expose missing type information; matching them can justify header changes and struct refinements that improve nearby larger functions.
When a function writes to memory passed by the caller, try a typed output parameter before assuming an unknown return value.
Ground callbacks should prefer Ground_GObj* plus GET_GROUND when they immediately access Ground state; using HSD_GObj* can hide useful type information and block matching.
Bitfield names and union members in shared stage structs can eliminate raw M2C_FIELD-style offset code for tiny flag operations.
Precise return types matter for predicates and accessors: bool for equality checks, int/s32 for integer field returns, and pointer types for dynamic-bones or item references.
Partial global struct declarations with explicit padding are acceptable evidence-grounded stepping stones when only a late field offset is known.
A tiny callee signature change can improve a larger unmatched caller, as shown by ftCo_CaptureKirby passing &vec1 into ftKb_SpecialN_800F5B20.
naming_conventions: Retained address-based unknown function names such as fn_800D769C, fn_8016A450, fn_8016A46C, and fn_80214658 where no semantic names were supplied.

Kept subsystem prefixes consistent: ftCo_ for common fighter code, ftKb_ for Kirby, gm_ for game mode, grGreens_/grKongo_/grOldKongo_ for stages, ifMagnify_ for interface, un_ for unknown utility, and hsd_ for sysdolphin baselib.
Used type aliases with subsystem meaning in prototypes, including Fighter_GObj*, Ground_GObj*, Item_GObj*, HSD_GObj*, MatchEnd*, and ArticleDynamicBones**.
Added struct names that mirror existing subsystem style, such as grGreens_GroundVars and un_80301C64_t.
For newly understood but still semantically unclear fields, preserved offset-style names such as xB4, xC0, xC6, x7C, x10, and x138.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Represent 28-byte predicate functions directly as equality/threshold comparisons, e.g. motion_id >= ftCo_MS_ItemScopeStartEmpty or lbl_80472C30.x7C == 0x14.
Use simple if-then assignments for tiny state transitions instead of switch or helper abstractions when the assembly only checks one field value.
For output stores, match the register/store pattern with a void function taking a typed pointer and assigning consecutive fields.
For global flag setters, write directly to the named global bitfield members, such as lbl_8046B488.unk_10_b3, unk_10_b4, and unk_10_b6.
For bitmask globals, preserve the exact mask/or expression; hsd_80396868 uses (x10 & 0x0FFFFFF0) | 0x80000000.
A small empty function was left with a size comment in grGreens_80214654, suggesting size accounting around adjacent tiny functions mattered for this sweep.
Padding a static/global struct to expose a known offset field can unblock small matches without claiming the full data layout.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased to 44.43%, +0.01%, +568 bytes.
The bot listed 18 new matches and one additional improvement: ftCo_800BD1DC in ftCo_CaptureKirby improved from 90.51% to 93.78%.
follow_up_queries: Find other unmatched 28-byte functions that are simple predicates, field accessors, or output-parameter stores.
Search for remaining UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes adjacent to newly typed Kirby, gm_1601, gm_17C0, stage, un, and particle functions.
Audit other Ground callbacks that still take HSD_GObj* but immediately use GET_GROUND and could be Ground_GObj*.
Look for additional uses of ftKb_FighterVars.xC0 now that it is typed as Item_GObj*.
Expand un_803FA258 and lbl_8040BC3C layouts only when future matched functions provide more field evidence.
